mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the cross-sectional IC drops below -0.05 during high unemployment spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in real GDP growth and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST rise simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity that outpaces demand growth, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 5.0% indicate restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions, immediately depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, or the signal fails to predict downward price movement in > 55% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and a flat unemployment rate signals labor market resilience that supports corporate hiring and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns decline when PAYEMS rises while UNRATE remains flat, or the joint signal produces a negative rank correlation with SPX returns across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real disposable personal income fuels consumer spending that directly lifts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or 60-day forward returns in high RSAFS growth periods are not statistically distinct from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings exceeding 3.0% alongside rising industrial output signal overheating that forces aggressive policy tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL and INDPRO rise simultaneously, or the signal fails to predict downward movement in > 55% of out-of-sample instances.
